methodology: Manual batch run — responses generated inline by Claude agents with/without skill loaded. Timing not captured per eval; agent-level totals recorded in summary.

failed_assertions: Null input is represented as a blank cell (not the string 'null') in the table
Response provides the tabletest dependency with groupId 'org.tabletest' (not guessed)

evals.name: Loan approval spec with open questions

failed_assertions: Uses value sets {PENDING, CONFIRMED} or equivalent to express 'regardless of which starting state' for cases where a rule holds across states
Table uses value-set syntax (curly braces like {READ, WRITE} or {READ, WRITE, DELETE}) to express rules that hold across multiple actions — rather than one row per role/action combination
Table has fewer than 9 data rows (i.e. does not enumerate every role/action combination individually)
CONTRACTOR is either placed in a separate @TableTest (not mixed with the level×department grid) OR the response explicitly notes the structural asymmetry and explains the design choice

evals.name: Shipping cost spec — blank vs value set

old_skill.vs_iteration_2: 6 failures on expanded eval set (evals 1-6 had 3 failures vs iter-2's 1)

analyst_notes: Eval 7 (permission check / value sets) — LARGEST GAP: old_skill scores 3/5 (60%) vs with_skill 5/5. Without skill guidance, the model enumerates all 9 role/action combinations individually, completely missing the {READ, WRITE} value-set syntax. This is the clearest demonstration of skill value in iteration 3.

Eval 9 (contractor structure) — SKILL-ATTRIBUTABLE: old_skill fails contractor-handled-separately-or-noted (4/5 vs 5/5). Without the skill's 'Match Table Structure to the Logic' guidance, the model mixed CONTRACTOR into the main grid without recognising the structural asymmetry.

Eval 2 old_skill — CHANGED FAILURE MODE: In iteration 2, old_skill failed type-conversion-addressed. In iteration 3, it fails null-as-blank-cell instead (and passes type-conversion-addressed). Single-run variance — both are real weaknesses the skill addresses.

Eval 3 old_skill — REGRESSION vs iteration 2: was 4/4 in iter 2, now 3/4. The agent guessed 'org.tabletest' correctly but flagged it as uncertain; graded as fail per the '(not guessed)' qualifier in the assertion text. Iter-2 grading may have been more lenient on confidence. Single-run variance.

Evals 10, 11 (new spec-by-example evals) — old_skill passes both at 100%. Multiple-table splitting and blank-vs-value-set distinction appear within the base model's capability without the skill. These evals are less discriminating for the current model.
Evals 4, 6, 8 — both configs pass at 100%, consistent with iteration 2. These are robustly covered by both configs.
with_skill maintains 100% across all 11 evals (56/56), demonstrating consistent performance on the expanded eval set.

Net skill advantage in iteration 3: +6 assertions (10.7pp gap). Largest contributors: eval 7 (value sets, 2 assertions), eval 9 (contractor structure, 1), eval 2 (null convention, 1), eval 3 (dependency confidence, 1), eval 5 (value sets, 1).
